final_text:
The canonical ranking confirms all 8 atom ranks are distinct (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7), meaning **all 8 carbons are in unique environments**. For the 1H NMR spectrum, this gives us:

| Carbon | H count | Environment |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| C3 (CH₂) | 2 diastereotopic H | Adjacent to ether O |
| C4 (CH) | 1 H | Bridgehead next to C3 |
| C5 (CH₂) | 2 diastereotopic H | Middle of 5-ring |
| C6 (CH) | 1 H | Bridgehead next to carbonyl C2 |
| C7 (CH₂) | 2 diastereotopic H | Bridge methylene |

The canonical ranks confirm all eight carbons are topologically distinct (no symmetry). Since the two protons on each CH₂ group (C3, C5, C7) are **diastereotopic** due to the chiral bicyclic framework, each proton is a **unique signal**.

## Final Answer: **8 distinct 1H NMR peaks**

The molecule **3-oxabicyclo[3.2.1]octan-2-one** (O=C1OCC2CC1C₂) has:

1. **No molecular symmetry** — the carbonyl (C2) and ether oxygen (O3) break any potential mirror plane
2. **5 carbons bearing protons** — C3 (CH₂), C4 (CH), C5 (CH₂), C6 (CH), C7 (CH₂)
3. **Diastereotopic CH₂ protons** — The 2 protons on each of C3, C5, and C7 are in different magnetic environments due to the chiral bicyclic framework
4. **Total: 8 unique proton environments**

This result is derived from both RDKit canonical ranking (all 8 carbon atoms have distinct ranks) and symmetry analysis of the bicyclic structure — no symmetry plane or rotation axis exists, so every proton experiences a unique magnetic environment.

| Signal # | Assignment | Type | Count |
|----------|-----------|------|-------|
| 1 | C3-Ha | diastereotopic CH₂ | 1H |
| 2 | C3-Hb | diastereotopic CH₂ | 1H |
| 3 | C4-H | bridgehead CH | 1H |
| 4 | C5-Ha | diastereotopic CH₂ | 1H |
| 5 | C5-Hb | diastereotopic CH₂ | 1H |
| 6 | C6-H | bridgehead CH | 1H |
| 7 | C7-Ha | diastereotopic CH₂ (bridge) | 1H |
| 8 | C7-Hb | diastereotopic CH₂ (bridge) | 1H |

**Answer: 8 peaks.**
